title: croton armstrongii
intro: Croton armstrongii, also known as the Devil's Backbone, is a succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its distinctive, branching stems and small, rounded leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Croton armstrongii include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce stress. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have therapeutic effects when consumed.
